sourceMovie.title: Love Hurts
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Love Hurts (2025) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jonathan Eusebio's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bird on a Wire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love Hurts clicked for you, Bird on a Wire (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love Hurts?
faq.answer: Try Bird on a Wire, Rush Hour 2, Rush Hour 3—each shares DNA with Love Hurts while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love Hurts?
faq.answer: Love Hurts (2025) is best described as Action, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love Hurts worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
